Description: Malicious SQL injected through user input to manipulate database queries
Prevention: Always use parameterized queries or ORM query builders. Never concatenate user input into SQL

Description: Malicious JavaScript injected through user input and rendered in the browser
Prevention: Escape all output by default. Use Blade {{ }} which auto-escapes. Sanitize rich text content

Description: Attacker tricks authenticated user into making unwanted requests to your application
Prevention: Include CSRF token in all forms. Laravel includes CSRF middleware by default for web routes

Description: Attacker adds unexpected fields to form submissions to modify protected database columns
Prevention: Define $fillable (whitelist) or $guarded (blacklist) on all Eloquent models

Description: Storing passwords in plaintext or with weak hashing exposes all users on database breach
Prevention: Use bcrypt or Argon2id via Hash facade. Never store plaintext passwords

Description: Failing to validate user input leads to unexpected data corrupting your application
Prevention: Validate ALL user input at the boundary using Form Requests or validate() method

Description: Uploaded files can contain malware or be executable scripts if not properly validated
Prevention: Validate file type size and extension. Store outside web root. Generate random filenames

Description: Without rate limiting attackers can brute-force logins or flood your API
Prevention: Apply rate limiting on authentication and API endpoints

Description: Hardcoding secrets or storing sensitive data unencrypted exposes it on code leak
Prevention: Store secrets in .env. Use Laravel Crypt for sensitive database fields. Never commit .env

Description: Missing security headers leave your application vulnerable to various attacks
Prevention: Add security headers via middleware or web server configuration

Description: Insecure session configuration enables session hijacking and fixation attacks
Prevention: Configure secure session settings in production

Description: Missing authorization checks allow users to access or modify resources they do not own
Prevention: Always check authorization. Use policies and form requests authorize()

Description: Overly permissive CORS settings allow any website to make requests to your API
Prevention: Configure specific allowed origins methods and headers

Description: Debug mode reveals stack traces environment variables and database details to users
Prevention: Set APP_DEBUG=false in production. Use custom error pages

Description: PHP unserialize() on untrusted data can lead to remote code execution
Prevention: Never use unserialize() on user input. Use JSON for data interchange

Description: User input in file paths allows reading arbitrary files on the server
Prevention: Validate file paths. Use basename(). Never use raw user input in file paths

Description: API keys exposed in URLs or client-side code can be stolen and abused
Prevention: Pass API keys in headers not URLs. Rotate keys regularly. Use scoped tokens

Description: Application makes HTTP requests to attacker-controlled URLs accessing internal services
Prevention: Validate and restrict URLs before making server-side HTTP requests

Description: Logging sensitive information exposes it in log files which may be less protected
Prevention: Sanitize sensitive fields before logging. Use hidden attributes on models

Description: Using packages with known security vulnerabilities puts your application at risk
Prevention: Regularly audit dependencies. Keep packages updated. Use security advisories
Code Good:
# Regular audit:
composer audit

# Update dependencies:
composer update --with-all-dependencies

# Use Roave security advisories:
composer require --dev roave/security-advisories:dev-latest
// This package prevents installing packages with known CVEs

# GitHub Dependabot:
// .github/dependabot.yml
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: 'composer'
    schedule:
      interval: 'weekly'
Code Bad:
# Never auditing:
composer install
# Running for years without updating
# Using packages with known CVEs
# No automated security scanning
